title: Module 1
content:
Ultrasound imaging: Properties of ultrasound- Doppler effect. Principle of
image formation- constructional details of US probes- Transducer Design –
Considerations-Transducer arrays- Modes of scanning-A mode-B mode-M
mode scanners-instrumentation-axial and lateral resolutions- Duplex
scanning-applications of each. Doppler effect- Principles of 3D/4D
ultrasound. (Assignment only -Functional Ultrasound Imaging, Clinical
applications- therapeutic uses of ultrasound)

title: Module 2
content:
Computed Tomography: Principle of CT imaging, Generations of
scanners-system instrumentation-X-ray tubes for CT applications-pencil
beam and cone beam projections-Image reconstruction algorithms for pencil
beam, Image characteristics-CT numbers-Nuclear imaging-PET-SPECT-
Rectilinear Scanner, Gamma camera

title: Module 3
content:
Magnetic Resonance Imaging: Principle of Nuclear Magnetic Resonance-
Relaxation-MR instrumentation-Contrast mechanisms in MRI- pulse
sequences- FLASH-GRASS-principle of image reconstruction, Spatial
encoding systems-   k-space - Functional MRI- BOLD - Spatial and temporal
Resolution of fMRI.  (Assignment only: diffusion weighted imaging, MR
spectroscopy)

title: Module 4
content:
Hybrid Imaging: Instrumentation MR-PET Instrumentation- MR
Compatible PET Detector Technology, MR-PET System Architecture. PET-
CT, SPECT-CT.  Intraoperative imaging Intraoperative imaging and image
guided therapy-X-Ray Hybrid Modalities for Image Guidance-Technology
of Ultrasound-Guided Therapy- CT   Guided Interventions: Current Practice
and Future Directions
